commands.review_summary:
The PR changes menu card item enablement and highlight reconciliation so non-clickable, non-submenu menu card rows stay disabled and do not remain visually highlighted after recycling.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and screenshots rather than a local app run: current main enables all menu card items, and the menu delegate highlights enabled custom rows. The PR screenshots also show the visible before/after hover behavior.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair job is needed; the branch is focused, has no blocking code finding, and should proceed through normal maintainer validation.

target.title: fix: don't highlight non-interactive menu card items on hover
target.body: ## Summary Avoid highlighting non-interactive menu card rows on hover by only marking `NSMenuItem`s enabled when they actually expose interaction. ## Implementation - Updated `Sources/CodexBar/StatusItemController+MenuCardItems.swift` - Replaced unconditional `item.isEnabled = true` with `item.isEnabled = submenu != nil || onClick != nil` - Applied the same rule in both the non-rendered menu path and the hosting- view path - Preserves submenu rows and clickable rows as enabled - Leaves informational rows disabled so AppKit does not treat them as hover- highlightable actions ## Why The previous implementation marked every menu card item as enabled, including rows that were purely informational. That caused AppKit to render them like interactive menu items on hover, which implied behavior that does not exist. ## Testing - Built the app locally - Tested the updated hover behavior in the app ## Screenshots Before: After:
target.author: elijahfriedman
